reason: The text describes how Gehazi, the servant of Elisha, deceitfully pursued Naaman after he was healed by Elisha, seeking gifts from him. Despite Elisha refusing to take any gifts from Naaman out of reverence for the LORD, Gehazi chose to run after Naaman and accept gifts from him. This act of receiving money from Naaman, an enemy of the Hebrews (as he was a Syrian), led to Gehazi being cursed with Naaman's leprosy as a consequence of his greed and dishonesty.
